tool.name: backtranseq
tool.summary: Back-translate protein records into deterministic representative DNA codons

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: `backtranseq` back-translates protein sequence records into one deterministic representative DNA coding sequence per residue using the shared EMBOSS-RS standard-code back-translation core. The v1 behavior is intentionally narrow and reproducible rather than biologically expansive.

sections.title: Inputs
sections.content: The current interface accepts one local protein-oriented sequence input path. Explicit nucleotide-classified records are rejected. Records classified as `unknown` remain acceptable when the shared loaders preserve protein-like residue content.

sections.title: Outputs
sections.content: The tool emits one DNA FASTA record per input protein record in stable order. Output identifiers are preserved, the output molecule kind is `dna`, and descriptions are annotated with the suffix `representative back-translated DNA`.

sections.title: Legacy Context
sections.content: This acceptance anchor keeps one historical-style `backtranseq` invocation in view and compares the EMBOSS-RS FASTA payload against a committed expected output. The comparison is intentionally scoped to deterministic representative codons under the standard genetic code.

sections.title: Current Status
sections.content: This method is implemented and exposed through `emboss-rs backtranseq`. Current validation covers the deterministic codon policy, stop-symbol handling with `* -> TAA`, and stable FASTA emission from committed protein fixtures.

sections.title: Caveats
sections.content: The first release uses one fixed representative DNA codon per residue and does not expose codon-usage weighting, alternate genetic codes, or RNA output. Unsupported residues fail clearly instead of being approximated.

artifacts.label: Protein back-translation fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed protein fixture used for deterministic representative back-translation validation.

examples.title: Back-translate protein records into deterministic representative DNA
examples.description: Back-translates the committed protein fixture into one representative DNA coding sequence per residue using the governed EMBOSS-RS standard-code policy.

expected_outputs.label: Representative DNA FASTA output
expected_outputs.description: A stable FASTA rendering of the deterministic representative DNA sequences.
